scope: ACL27 supplement page 30 navigation media atlas
root_cause: The bottom navigation index panels previously treated wide source index sheets as large crops. Under paper-scale placement this made selected rows look compressed, left a partial-row risk, and contradicted the updated AI-slot composer rule to split wide strips into semantic segments before containing them.
changes: Added a regression check requiring the page-30 navigation atlas to use row-card composers and to avoid the old single wide crop.
Replaced the bottom selected6 panel with deterministic pair row cards for cases 493_493 and 484_484.
Replaced the bottom 0036/0066 panel with deterministic row-detail cards for four complete official still rows and key original/noMDL start/end cells.
Preserved contain/letterbox scaling for every row/cell crop; no cover-style placement is used in build_navigation_atlas.

local_visual_review.reviewer: Codex local visual review using render-visual-reviewer paper figure protocol; no subagent spawned because this environment only permits spawn_agent when explicitly requested.
local_visual_review.standalone_verdict: PASS for clipping/containment. The bottom row cards are complete, labels are inside their lanes, and no cell crosses a card boundary.
local_visual_review.pdf_page_30_verdict: PASS for paper-scale placement. The figure is fully contained on the page, the caption does not collide with the image, and the bottom row cards remain readable as compact context.
local_visual_review.residual_caveat: The upper 0036/0066 readable route panel remains intentionally small because it is context; larger paired case pages below remain the main qualitative still evidence.

follow_up_risks: Pages 22-23 and 33-38 remain dense visual pages and should be kept in the broader supplement visual-review loop.
The broader polish goal remains active: this record only closes the page-30 index-row clipping/scale issue.
